endpoints.purpose: Read and change the router admin login account/password
Configure password recovery via email (SMTP) settings
Get/set local management access mode (all devices vs specified)
Enable/disable HTTPS for local web management
Configure remote (WAN) web management enable/port/allowed managers
Manage the list of devices allowed for local management (allow-device grid store)
Check whether the device is at factory default / whether cloud account ever logged in
Read virtual server / port-forwarding entries (used to detect port conflicts with remote mgmt)
Read and set the active DDNS service provider (TP-Link / No-IP / DynDNS) and DDNS enable state.
Manage TP-Link cloud DDNS domains: list registered domains/status, register (insert) a domain, remove a domain, and bind/unbind a domain to this device.
Configure and control the DynDNS provider session: read status, login with credentials, logout, and refresh the DDNS update.
Configure and control the No-IP provider session: read status, login with credentials, logout, and refresh the DDNS update.
Check whether the router has internet connectivity to the TP-Link cloud before performing DDNS domain operations.
Read and configure the LAN DHCP server (enable, address pool range, lease time, gateway, DNS).
Manage DHCP address reservation entries (list, add, edit, toggle enable, delete MAC-to-IP bindings).
Read the list of currently connected/leased DHCP clients.
Run network diagnostics (ping / traceroute / nslookup) against a target host and poll for streaming results.
Read and configure LAN/WAN port mirroring: enable flag, the mirroring (destination) port, and the set of source ports to reflect from.
Read and configure the DMZ host (enable flag and the LAN IP address exposed to the internet).
Read NAT global setting to check whether NAT is enabled (gates the DMZ feature UI).
Query firmware upgrade progress percent (firmwareUpgradeProxy load and checkUpgradeStatusProxy poll)
Upload a local .bin firmware file to flash the router (manual upgrade)
Read and configure automatic firmware auto-update enable state and schedule time (autoUpgradeModel load/submit)
List mesh/OneMesh satellite devices and trigger/monitor their firmware upgrade
Load latest online firmware info from cloud and trigger the online cloud firmware upgrade/download (onlineUpgradeProxy)
Check cloud for available upgrade number/availability (checkUpgradeNumberProxy)
Verify the device is registered/recognized by the cloud before upgrade (checkDeviceProxy / detectDevice)
Check cloud account/server connection status (checkCloudConnectionProxy)
Poll firmware download progress percent during online cloud upgrade (checkDownloadStatusProxy)
Read WAN wire connection type (wired/wireless) used to decide reconnect flow (checkWireTypeProxy)
Read/write the guest network SSID, encryption, PSK, portal auth and redirect settings (2.4G band, part of the combined guest model proxy).
Read/write guest network SSID/encryption/PSK settings for the 5G band (part of the combined guest model proxy).
Read/write guest network SSID/encryption/PSK settings for the second 5G band on tri-band devices (part of combined guest proxy).
Unified guest network config (SSID/encryption/PSK/portal) applied across all bands; the primary form the guestNetwork model reads and submits.
Read/write guest network permission flags: client isolation and access to local network.
Read/write the captive portal login page content (welcome title, terms text, font/theme colors and opacity).
Read/write per-band guest network upload/download bandwidth limits.
Read/write the guest network access effective-time limit (none/4h/1day/custom) and report remaining time.
Read the 2.4G host wireless enable/disabled status (used to gate whether the 2.4G guest toggle is available).
Read the 5G host wireless enable/disabled status to gate the 5G guest toggle.
Read the second 5G host wireless enable/disabled status (tri-band) to gate that guest toggle.
Read the 6G host wireless enable/disabled status to gate the 6G guest toggle.
Read 2.4G system parameters (WDS enable) to detect a WDS conflict that blocks the guest network.
Read 5G system parameters (WDS enable) to detect a WDS conflict blocking the guest network.
Read second-5G system parameters (WDS enable) to detect a WDS conflict blocking the guest network.
Read 6G system parameters (WDS enable) to detect a WDS conflict blocking the guest network.
Read the current captive-portal logo image path and upload a new logo image.
Read the current captive-portal background image path and upload a new background image.
Proxy for guest folder/media sharing config, kept in sync with the guest network SSID/security settings.
List the WAN IPv4 connection protocols supported by the device (dynamic, static, pppoe, l2tp, pptp, bigpond, dslite, v6plus, ocn).
Read current WAN IPv4 status: whether the connection is enabled, the active conntype, and autodetect flag.
Read LAN/WAN IPv4 address summary (LAN IP/mask, WAN IP/mask, WAN MAC).
Configure/read Dynamic IP (DHCP) WAN; also renew or release the DHCP lease.
Configure/read Static IP WAN connection settings.
Configure/read PPPoE WAN; also dial connect/disconnect and renew/release the secondary connection.
Control PPPoE credential auto-retrieval; the 'stop' api sends operation:write with disabled:true to stop retrieval.
Configure/read L2TP VPN WAN; also dial connect and disconnect.
Configure/read PPTP VPN WAN; also dial connect and disconnect.
Configure/read BigPond (Telstra Cable) WAN connection settings.
Configure/read DS-Lite WAN (AFTR address, dynamic vs manual mode).
Configure/read v6plus (IPv4-over-IPv6) WAN connection.
Configure/read OCN Virtual Connect (Japan) WAN connection.
Configure/read WAN MAC clone (default MAC, custom MAC, or use current computer's MAC).
Read/toggle NAT and hardware NAT boost (turbo) on the WAN.
Read/set the current WAN port link speed / duplex negotiation.
List the WAN port speed/duplex options supported by the device.
Read the configured WAN port and detect/probe physical link state on a selected port.
Read current IPv6 internet/WAN connection status (enable state and connection type)
Read/configure LAN-side IPv6 settings (address assignment type, DHCPv6/SLAAC/RDNSS prefixes, release time)
Configure dynamic (DHCPv6/auto) IPv6 WAN connection and renew/release the lease
Configure static IPv6 WAN connection (address, gateway, DNS, MTU)
Configure PPPoE IPv6 WAN connection and connect/disconnect the session
Configure 6to4 tunnel IPv6 WAN connection and connect/disconnect
Configure 6rd IPv6 WAN connection (IPv4 mask length, 6rd prefix/prefix length, relay IPv4)
Configure DS-Lite IPv6 WAN connection (dynamic/static/pppoe secondary conn) and renew/release/connect/disconnect
Configure IPv6 pass-through / bridge mode connection
Read list of supported IPv6 WAN connection protocol types (name/value options)
Read/configure MAC address clone for the IPv6 WAN connection
Manage IPv6 firewall rules / port forwarding (fr6) entries
Read list of IPv6 LAN clients (for firewall rule target selection)
Read and set the router LAN IPv4 address and subnet mask (advanced LAN settings).
Read current WAN/LAN IPv4 status (address, netmask, gateway, DNS, conn type) used to validate LAN IP conflicts.
Read and configure LAN link aggregation (enable, LACP/static mode, hash mode, per-port membership).
Reboot the router (triggered when LAN IP or link-agg changes require a restart); response returns reboot_time.
Read or toggle the router status LED on/off (general LED control switch)
Read or configure LED Night Mode (scheduled window during which the LED is dimmed/off)
Read the connected-clients list (device info or live speed) driving the network map
blockClientProxy - block/deprioritize a device via device-priority (writes state)
clientStatusProxy - load per-client status snapshot
changeDeviceNameProxy - rename a device (set its alias/name)
getSpeedLimitMaxProxy/speedLimitModel - read max allowed speed and set per-client download/upload bandwidth limits
onlineDeviceStore/blockDevice - load blacklist of blocked devices and add (block) a device to the access-control blacklist
Reads the LAN IPv4 network-map info (used in AP/bridge mode to show the router's LAN address and mask)
Reads speed-test availability/status for the internet network-map panel (getSpeedTestStatusProxy.getStatus)
Read and edit the router's display/device name shown in the network map.
Read router status: CPU/memory usage, storage, ethernet ports, and per-band wireless status/encryption.
Read/toggle the 2.4GHz main wireless radio on or off.
Read/toggle the 5GHz (or 5GHz-1) main wireless radio on or off.
Read/toggle the second 5GHz main wireless radio (tri-band) on or off.
Read/toggle the 6GHz (WiFi 6E) main wireless radio on or off.
Read/toggle the 2.4GHz guest network on or off.
Read/toggle the 5GHz (or 5GHz-1) guest network on or off.
Read/toggle the second 5GHz guest network (tri-band) on or off.
Read/toggle the 6GHz (WiFi 6E) guest network on or off.

endpoints.form: iot_2g (combined with iot_5g and, on tri-band, iot_5g_2)
endpoints.purpose: Read/toggle the per-band IoT networks and their encryption/PSK keys (URL assembled dynamically; 5g_2 appended only on tri-band).
Read full WAN/modem connection status (IPv4 config, DNS, uptime, LTE modem signal) for the network status page.
Read internet reachability status (connected / disconnected / connecting / unplugged / poor connection) for the status indicator.
Read the current router operation mode and its supported modes, and switch the operation mode (router/ap/repeater/client/multissid), which reboots the device.
Manage NAT virtual-server / port-forwarding rules (list, create, edit, enable toggle, delete).
List LAN-connected devices to pick an IP address when adding a port-forwarding rule.
Read/write the scheduled auto-reboot configuration (enable, repeat cycle, time, day)
Immediately reboot the router device
Read the system log entries; clear the log; and trigger sending the log by email.
Load the list of available log type categories used to filter the log.
Read and set the current log display filter (by type and level).
Read and configure the log-by-email settings (SMTP server, credentials, auto-mail schedule).
Save/download the system log to a local file (file upload/export operation).
Read and write router system time settings (NTP servers, timezone, manual date/time, mode); gmt/refresh acquire time from an NTP/GMT server.
Read and configure Daylight Saving Time rules (enable, start/end month/week/day/hour) and view DST status.
Read/set whether the device supports and uses 24-hour time format.
Read the timezone list/selection used during quick setup (read-only proxy, writeFilter forces operation:read).
Read and set the wireless region/country, including whether region selection is permitted and the available region list.
Read NAT enable state (used to gate UPnP availability since UPnP depends on NAT).
Read and toggle whether the UPnP service is enabled on the router.
List active UPnP port-mapping entries created by clients (name, IP, ports, protocol).
Read and write 2.4GHz wireless radio settings (SSID, security, channel, txpower, MU-MIMO).
Read and write 5GHz (primary) wireless radio settings.
Read and write second 5GHz band radio settings (tri-band devices).
Read and write 6GHz band radio settings (WiFi 6E devices).
Read the router's regulatory region/country and its wireless capability descriptor (also chained onto each band read).
Read/write Smart Connect (single unified SSID across bands) enable state.
Read/write OFDMA feature enable state.
Read/write TWT (Target Wake Time) feature enable state.
Read 2.4GHz system parameters (radio enable state used to gate channel selection).
Read primary 5GHz system parameters (radio enable state).
Read second 5GHz band system parameters (radio enable state).
Read 6GHz band system parameters (radio enable state).
Read/write Amazon FFS (Frustration-Free Setup) enable configuration.
Read and configure the wireless off schedule (enable flag, per-slot off-time windows with repeat, and next wireless on/off time).
Manage the saved Wake-on-LAN device list (list/add/edit/delete entries) and send a magic packet to wake a device.
Load the list of currently connected/known devices so the user can pick one to add to the WOL list.
Read and toggle the WPS feature on/off (global WPS enable) with an activation wait_time.
Read/manage the router's WPS PIN: read PIN and per-band lock state, toggle router-PIN enable (wps_label), and write to generate a new PIN or restore the default PIN.
Drive the WPS connection handshake: read status/timeout, start PBC or PIN connect, poll pbc/pin status, and cancel an in-progress WPS session.
Read 2.4GHz wireless settings to determine whether WPS is usable (SSID enabled, hidden, encryption/cipher).
Read 5GHz wireless settings to determine whether WPS is usable (SSID enabled, hidden, encryption/cipher).
Read the second 5GHz band settings (tri-band routers) to determine whether WPS is usable.
